name: TiDB Cloud API
what_it_does: Distributed SQL database platform (cloud) with REST API — providing MySQL-compatible HTAP (Hybrid Transactional and Analytical Processing) database with serverless and dedicated tiers, horizontal scaling, and strong consistency for applications requiring both OLTP and OLAP from a single database.
use_cases: Provisioning and managing TiDB Serverless clusters via REST API for dev/staging
Querying and modifying data via TiDB Cloud Data Service API (SQL over HTTP)
Managing cluster configuration, scaling, and backups programmatically
Monitoring cluster metrics and alerting via TiDB Cloud monitoring API
Importing and exporting data to/from TiDB Cloud via data API
not_for: Document databases or non-relational data (TiDB is relational SQL)
Simple single-node MySQL (use RDS or PlanetScale for simpler needs)
best_when: A company needs MySQL-compatible database with horizontal scaling, both OLTP and OLAP from one system, and serverless auto-scaling for variable workloads.
avoid_when: You need simple single-node MySQL without horizontal scaling, document storage, or have very small workloads where serverless cold starts are acceptable.

auth.notes: API key (public key + private key pair) for management API. Digest auth using key pair. Data Service API uses separate API key. OpenAPI 3.0 spec available. Scoped access for project, organization, and data operations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Serverless from $0.10/RU; Dedicated from $0.208/hour per vCPU; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Generous serverless free tier. RU (Request Unit) pricing for serverless. Per-vCPU pricing for dedicated. Chinese company (PingCAP, Beijing). Multi-cloud: AWS, GCP. Data residency in US, EU, AP. Strong open-source community (TiDB is MIT licensed). 3,000+ enterprise customers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST management API and Data Service API (SQL over HTTP). Digest auth with key pair. OpenAPI spec. Webhook for cluster events. Good documentation. Two API surfaces: management (cluster ops) and Data Service (SQL queries). MySQL-compatible — standard SQL libraries work.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Database queries fully idempotent (reads). Cluster operations idempotent by cluster ID. Data writes follow standard SQL transaction semantics. Backup operations idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Digest auth (public key + private key pair) — not standard Bearer token
Two separate APIs: management API and Data Service API — different endpoints
Chinese company — data sovereignty considerations for sensitive data
Serverless cold start latency — not suitable for latency-critical operations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA certified. GDPR compliant. Digest auth with key pair. Multi-region data residency (US, EU, AP). Open-source database core (TiDB MIT license). Chinese parent company — verify data residency per compliance requirements.
